hypothesis: If E2EFOLD is trained end-to-end with a joint objective that couples structure prediction and structure-based sequence design using the composite loss, then the designed sequences will achieve higher sequence recovery and the predicted structures of these sequences will more closely match reference structures (higher TM-score and lower RMSD) than those produced by Boltz-2 and standard baselines, because the end-to-end optimization enforces compatibility between amino acid identities and three-dimensional geometry.
